requirements.title: Goroutine termination signal check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (ANA001) for every goroutine body that contains no channel receive, select, or context.Done() reference, indicating the goroutine has no mechanism to receive a termination signal.

requirements.title: Goroutine-in-loop detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (ANA002) when a go statement appears inside a for or range loop body, indicating unbounded goroutine creation.

requirements.title: Blocking sleep in goroutine detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (ANA003) when a time.Sleep call is found inside a goroutine body, indicating a blocking pattern that prevents cooperative cancellation.

requirements.title: Defer-in-loop detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (ANA004) when a defer statement appears inside a for or range loop body, indicating deferred cleanup that will not execute until the enclosing function returns.

requirements.title: Missing config file returns ErrNoConfig
requirements.text: config.Load shall return an error wrapping fusa.ErrNoConfig when the specified .fusa.json file does not exist, allowing callers to distinguish absence from malformation.

requirements.title: Malformed config JSON returns error
requirements.text: config.Load shall return a non-nil error when .fusa.json contains syntactically invalid JSON, preventing silent use of a corrupted configuration.

requirements.title: Missing version field returns ErrInvalidConfig
requirements.text: config.Validate shall return an error wrapping fusa.ErrInvalidConfig when the version field is empty or absent.

requirements.title: Unsupported report format returns ErrInvalidConfig
requirements.text: config.Validate shall return an error wrapping fusa.ErrInvalidConfig when report.format is set to a value other than the empty string, 'text', or 'json'.

requirements.title: Default config is valid and uses safe defaults
requirements.text: config.Default shall return a Config with version='1', standard='generic', and report.format='text', which must pass Validate without error.

requirements.title: Config round-trips through JSON save and load
requirements.text: A Config written by config.Save and read back by config.Load shall be identical to the original, preserving all fields including Rules.Exclude, Project.ASIL, and Project.SIL.

requirements.title: Excluded rule IDs are skipped during engine run
requirements.text: Rule IDs listed in Config.Rules.Exclude shall not execute during engine.Registry.Run; their findings shall be absent from the result.

requirements.title: Per-rule severity override in configuration
requirements.text: Config.Rules.Severity shall be a map from rule ID to severity string ('ERROR', 'WARNING', or 'INFO'); after a rule executes, any finding whose rule ID appears in this map shall have its severity replaced with the configured value.

requirements.title: No-subcommand invocation prints usage and exits non-zero
requirements.text: When gofusa is invoked with no arguments, the CLI shall print a usage message and exit with a non-zero status code.

requirements.title: Unknown subcommand exits non-zero
requirements.text: When gofusa is invoked with an unrecognised subcommand, the CLI shall print an error message and exit with a non-zero status code.

requirements.title: Help command prints usage and exits zero
requirements.text: gofusa help (and --help, -h) shall print usage information that includes the tool name and available subcommands, and exit with status 0.

requirements.title: Version command prints version string and exits zero
requirements.text: gofusa version shall print a string containing the tool name and semver version, and exit with status 0.

requirements.title: Check exits zero when no ERROR findings are present
requirements.text: gofusa check shall exit with status 0 when the engine produces no findings of ERROR severity.

requirements.title: Check exits one when any ERROR finding is present
requirements.text: gofusa check shall exit with status 1 when the engine produces at least one finding of ERROR severity, signalling a blocking safety violation to CI pipelines.

requirements.title: Qualify exits zero and writes report when all cases pass
requirements.text: gofusa qualify shall exit with status 0 and write qualify-report.json when every built-in qualification case passes.

requirements.title: gofusa lint runs only lint rules
requirements.text: gofusa lint shall run only rules whose ID begins with 'LINT', producing the same output format as gofusa check and accepting the same --dir, --format, --output, and --strict flags.

requirements.title: gofusa analyze runs only analyze rules
requirements.text: gofusa analyze shall run only rules whose ID begins with 'ANA', producing the same output format as gofusa check and accepting the same --dir, --format, --output, and --strict flags.

requirements.title: gofusa template generates safety document templates
requirements.text: gofusa template shall generate one or more safety document templates (SAFETY_PLAN.md, TEST_EVIDENCE.md, HARA.md) in the specified output directory, accepting --dir and --type flags.

requirements.title: --strict flag exits non-zero on WARNING or ERROR findings
requirements.text: When gofusa check (or any filtered subcommand) is invoked with --strict, it shall exit with status 1 when any finding of WARNING or ERROR severity is present, even if no ERROR-only findings exist.

requirements.title: gofusa safety-case generates safety case files
requirements.text: gofusa safety-case shall write safety-case.json (machine-readable), safety-case.md (Markdown), and safety-case.mermaid (GSN diagram) to the output directory, accepting --dir, --output-dir, and --standard flags.

requirements.title: Safety case presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (SAFETYCASE001) when no safety-case.json safety case document is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: Safety case Build collects evidence items from project root
requirements.text: safetycase.Build shall read evidence files (check-report.json, .fusa-reqs.json, .fusa-evidence.json, qualify-report.json, sbom.json, provenance.json) from projectRoot and return a SafetyCase with an EvidenceItem for each file indicating its presence status and detail.

requirements.title: SafetyCase gaps slice lists absent evidence IDs
requirements.text: The Gaps field of a SafetyCase shall contain the ID of every EvidenceItem whose Status is absent; the slice shall be empty when all evidence is present.

requirements.title: Render supports text, JSON, and Mermaid formats
requirements.text: safetycase.Render shall write the safety case as Markdown when format is 'text', as JSON when format is 'json' or empty, and as a Mermaid flowchart when format is 'mermaid'; it shall return an error for any other value.

requirements.title: Mermaid output uses G/S/Sn node prefixes and hierarchical connections
requirements.text: The Mermaid output shall use G-prefixed nodes for goals, S-prefixed nodes for strategies, and Sn-prefixed nodes for solutions; the top goal G1 shall connect to the strategy S1 which connects to all sub-goals.

requirements.title: Compliance mapping for iso26262, iec61508, iso21434, and generic standards
requirements.text: safetycase.Build shall include compliance clause mappings for the configured standard; supported values are 'iso26262', 'iec61508', 'iso21434', and 'generic'; unrecognised values shall default to generic mappings.

requirements.title: Rules execute in deterministic ID-sorted order
requirements.text: engine.Registry.Rules shall return rules sorted lexicographically by ID, ensuring that rule execution order and report output are deterministic across runs.

requirements.title: Rule execution errors are collected without aborting the run
requirements.text: When a rule's Run method returns a non-nil error, the engine shall record the error in Result.Errors and continue executing remaining rules rather than aborting the check.

requirements.title: HasErrors reports true when any finding has ERROR severity
requirements.text: engine.Result.HasErrors shall return true if and only if at least one Finding in the result carries SeverityError.

requirements.title: MustRegister panics on duplicate rule ID
requirements.text: engine.Registry.MustRegister shall panic when called with a rule whose ID is already registered, making duplicate registration a hard failure detectable at program startup.

requirements.title: Register returns error for nil rule
requirements.text: engine.Registry.Register shall return a non-nil error when called with a nil rule argument.

requirements.title: Context cancellation halts rule execution
requirements.text: engine.Registry.Run shall stop executing rules and return promptly when the supplied context is cancelled, enabling timeout and cancellation support in CI pipelines.

requirements.title: RunFilter runs only rules matching a keep predicate
requirements.text: engine.Registry.RunFilter shall accept a keep func(Rule) bool predicate and execute only rules for which keep returns true, enabling focused rule subsets such as lint-only or analyze-only runs.

requirements.title: ErrNoConfig is the sentinel for absent configuration
requirements.text: fusa.ErrNoConfig shall be a non-nil sentinel error that callers can test with errors.Is to distinguish a missing .fusa.json from other I/O or parse errors.

requirements.title: ErrInvalidConfig is the sentinel for malformed configuration
requirements.text: fusa.ErrInvalidConfig shall be a non-nil sentinel error that callers can test with errors.Is to identify configuration validation failures.

requirements.title: ErrCheckFailed is the sentinel for ERROR-severity check failures
requirements.text: fusa.ErrCheckFailed shall be a non-nil sentinel error representing a check run in which one or more ERROR-severity findings were produced, suitable for wrapping in higher-level error values.

requirements.title: Configuration file presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report an ERROR finding (FUSA001) when no .fusa.json configuration file is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: Go module file presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report an ERROR finding (FUSA002) when no go.mod file is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: License file presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (FUSA003) when no LICENSE file is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: README file presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (FUSA004) when no README file is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: CI configuration presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (FUSA005) when no CI workflow file is found under .github/workflows/.

requirements.title: Discarded error return detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (LINT001) for every multi-value assignment where a blank identifier discards an error return value.

requirements.title: Panic call detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (LINT002) for every panic() call found in non-test Go source files.

requirements.title: Recover call inventory
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (LINT003) for every recover() call found in Go source files, providing an inventory of panic recovery points.

requirements.title: Unsafe package import detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (LINT004) for every import of the 'unsafe' package.

requirements.title: Reflect package import inventory
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (LINT005) for every import of the 'reflect' package, providing an inventory of reflection usage.

requirements.title: Global mutable variable detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (LINT006) for every package-level var declaration, providing an inventory of global mutable state.

requirements.title: No external runtime dependencies
requirements.text: The go-FuSa module shall declare no external runtime dependencies in go.mod. All functionality shall be implemented using the Go standard library only, ensuring supply-chain integrity.

requirements.title: All timestamps use UTC
requirements.text: All GeneratedAt timestamps in reports, evidence bundles, SBOMs, provenance records, and qualification reports shall use UTC, ensuring consistent interpretation across time zones.

requirements.title: Recognised safety standard identifiers
requirements.text: The config.Standard type shall accept the identifiers ISO26262, IEC61508, ISO21434, DO178C, and generic, covering the principal functional safety and cybersecurity standards.

requirements.title: Tool qualification report presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (QUALIFY001) when no qualify-report.json tool qualification report is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: Qualification report structure
requirements.text: A qualification report produced by qualify.Run shall include Go version, UTC generation timestamp, total/passed/failed case counts, and the full per-case result list.

requirements.title: Qualification cases run in isolated temporary directories
requirements.text: Each qualification test case shall execute in its own isolated OS temporary directory that is created before the case runs and deleted after it completes, preventing cross-case contamination.

requirements.title: Qualification report integrity hash
requirements.text: The qualification report shall include a Hash field containing the SHA-256 digest (lowercase hex, 64 characters) of the report JSON excluding the Hash field itself, enabling tampering detection.

requirements.title: SBOM presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (RELEASE001) when no sbom.json Software Bill of Materials is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: Build provenance presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (RELEASE002) when no provenance.json build provenance record is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: SBOM includes module path, Go version, and direct dependencies
requirements.text: release.BuildSBOM shall produce an SBOM that includes the module path from go.mod, the Go toolchain version from go.mod, and all direct dependencies listed in the require block.

requirements.title: SBOM components include dependency name, version, and hash
requirements.text: Each Component in the SBOM shall include the dependency module path, the declared version, and the h1: hash from go.sum where available.

requirements.title: Provenance record includes build environment fields
requirements.text: release.BuildProvenance shall produce a record that includes module path, Go runtime version, GOOS, and GOARCH, providing a complete build environment fingerprint.

requirements.title: SBOM and provenance serialise as valid JSON
requirements.text: release.SaveJSON shall write any SBOM or Provenance value as indented, valid JSON that can be parsed by a standard JSON decoder.

requirements.title: SBOM output conforms to SPDX 3.0.1 JSON-LD format
requirements.text: release.ToSPDX31 shall convert an SBOM to an SPDX 3.0.1 JSON-LD document containing a @context pointing to the SPDX 3.0.1 context URL and a @graph with CreationInfo, Tool, SpdxDocument, and software_Package elements.

requirements.title: Artifact manifest records SHA-256 hashes for all release files
requirements.text: release.BuildManifest shall accept a list of file paths and produce a Manifest containing the path and SHA-256 hex digest for each file, failing if any file cannot be read.

requirements.title: Text report includes rule ID, severity, and location
requirements.text: The text-format report shall include for each finding: the rule ID, severity label, and location in file:line:column format when line and column are present.

requirements.title: Text report shows PASS or FAIL result line
requirements.text: The text-format report shall end with 'Result: PASS' when there are no ERROR-severity findings, and 'Result: FAIL' when at least one ERROR-severity finding exists.

requirements.title: Report summary counts match finding severities
requirements.text: report.New shall compute Summary.Total, Errors, Warnings, and Infos by counting findings in the provided slice; the counts shall equal the actual numbers of findings at each severity.

requirements.title: JSON report is valid parseable JSON
requirements.text: The json-format report shall produce output that can be decoded by a standard JSON decoder into a report.Report struct without error.

requirements.title: JSON report includes all required top-level fields
requirements.text: The json-format report shall include the fields generatedAt, projectRoot, findings, and summary at the top level.

requirements.title: Requirements manifest presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (TRACE001) when no .fusa-reqs.json requirements manifest is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: Requirement implementation traceability check
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (TRACE002) for every requirement in .fusa-reqs.json that has no matching //fusa:req implementation tag in any Go source file.

requirements.title: TracedRequirements count is accurate
requirements.text: The Coverage.TracedRequirements field of a traceability matrix shall equal the number of requirements in the manifest that have at least one //fusa:req KindImpl tag in the source tree.

requirements.title: TestedRequirements count is accurate
requirements.text: The Coverage.TestedRequirements field of a traceability matrix shall equal the number of requirements in the manifest that have at least one //fusa:test KindTest tag in the source tree.

requirements.title: Scanner excludes vendor, testdata, and hidden directories
requirements.text: trace.ScanTags shall not scan Go files inside vendor/, testdata/, or any directory whose name begins with a dot, preventing false annotations from dependencies or generated files.

requirements.title: Traceability matrix renders in text and JSON formats
requirements.text: trace.Render shall produce a human-readable text format when format is 'text' and a machine-parseable JSON format when format is 'json', returning an error for unknown formats.

requirements.title: Annotations with empty requirement ID are silently skipped
requirements.text: A //fusa:req or //fusa:test annotation with no requirement ID after the prefix shall be silently ignored by trace.ScanTags and shall not produce a tag.

requirements.title: Test evidence bundle presence check
requirements.text: The tool shall report an INFO finding (VERIFY001) when no .fusa-evidence.json test evidence bundle is present in the project root directory.

requirements.title: Test failure detection
requirements.text: The tool shall report a WARNING finding (VERIFY002) when the .fusa-evidence.json bundle records one or more failed tests.

requirements.title: Evidence bundle includes provenance fields
requirements.text: An evidence bundle produced by verify.New shall include the project root path, Go runtime version, and a UTC generation timestamp.

requirements.title: Evidence bundle records per-test detail
requirements.text: Each entry in the evidence bundle Results slice shall include the test function name, package path, pass/fail/skip status, and elapsed time in seconds.

requirements.title: verify.Run invokes go test with JSON output
requirements.text: verify.Run shall execute go test -json -count=1 ./... in the specified directory, producing machine-readable output that is parsed into structured test results.

requirements.title: Full pipeline executes successfully on a minimal Go project
requirements.text: The complete gofusa pipeline (init, check, trace, verify, release, qualify) shall execute in sequence on a minimal Go project and produce all required output artifacts: .fusa.json, .fusa-evidence.json, sbom.json, provenance.json, and qualify-report.json.

requirements.title: dFMEA scanner discovers exported functions from Go source
requirements.text: fmea.Scan shall walk all non-vendor, non-testdata Go source files in the project root and produce one Entry per exported function declaration, including component name, function name, and severity.

requirements.title: Failure modes derived from return types and goroutine usage
requirements.text: fmea.Scan shall derive failure modes and effects heuristically: functions returning error receive 'Returns unexpected error', functions launching goroutines receive 'Goroutine not terminated', and severity is elevated to 'high' when a //fusa:req annotation is present.

requirements.title: RequirementIDs extracted from //fusa:req annotations
requirements.text: fmea.Scan shall parse the doc comment of each exported function and extract all requirement IDs from //fusa:req lines, associating them with the corresponding Entry.

requirements.title: dFMEA report exported in JSON and CSV formats
requirements.text: fmea.Render shall write the Report in 'json' format (indented JSON) or 'csv' format (RFC 4180 with header row: Component, Function, FailureModes, Effects, Severity, DetectionControl, RequirementIDs).

requirements.title: FMEA001 engine rule fires when fmea.json is absent
requirements.text: The FMEA001 engine rule shall emit an INFO finding when fmea.json does not exist in the project root, prompting the user to run 'gofusa fmea'.

requirements.title: Scanner builds package dependency graph from imports
requirements.text: boundary.Scan shall walk all non-vendor, non-testdata Go source files and construct a directed graph of package import relationships within the module, producing one Node per package and one Edge per import relationship.

requirements.title: Exported types and functions identified per package node
requirements.text: boundary.Scan shall collect all exported function names (suffix '()') and exported type names from each package and attach them to the corresponding Node as the Exports slice.

requirements.title: Boundary diagram rendered in Mermaid flowchart format
requirements.text: boundary.Render with format 'mermaid' shall produce a Mermaid flowchart LR diagram with one node per package and directed edges representing import dependencies.

requirements.title: Boundary diagram rendered in DOT (Graphviz) format
requirements.text: boundary.Render with format 'dot' shall produce a valid Graphviz DOT digraph with rankdir=LR, one node per package, and directed edges representing import dependencies.

requirements.title: BOUNDARY001 engine rule fires when boundary.mermaid is absent
requirements.text: The BOUNDARY001 engine rule shall emit an INFO finding when boundary.mermaid does not exist in the project root, prompting the user to run 'gofusa boundary'.

requirements.title: gofusa fmea generates dFMEA JSON and CSV artifacts
requirements.text: The 'gofusa fmea' command shall scan the project root, generate fmea.json and fmea.csv in the output directory, and print a summary of entry counts by severity.

requirements.title: gofusa boundary generates Mermaid and DOT boundary diagrams
requirements.text: The 'gofusa boundary' command shall scan the project root, generate boundary.mermaid and boundary.dot in the output directory, and print a summary of package and edge counts.
